name: Local HTML Browser

releases.author: pranavshantagiri
releases.description:
Release: Local HTML Browser 1.1.0
Requires Obsidian 1.12.7+ · Desktop only

What’s in 1.1.0 (since 1.0.3)
History — saved, searchable panel, delete/clear
Session restore — tabs + page tabs on startup
Tab bar — rename, drag, shortcuts, context menu
Standalone page tabs — Open as page, .webpage notes, no blank screen
Reliability — deferred loading, layout sync, history/tab fixes
Obsidian 1.12.7 — store installs latest (not stuck on 1.0.1)
releases.name: Local HTML Browser 1.1.0

releases.author: pranavshantagiri
releases.description:
Changes in 1.0.3
Second pass of Obsidian review fixes. No new features.

Requirements
minAppVersion raised from 1.7.2 → 1.13.0 (declarative settings)
Settings
Migrated to getSettingDefinitions() (searchable in Obsidian settings)
Removed deprecated display()
Removed plugin name and “General” headings
API / lint compliance
DOM via doc.win.createDiv() / createEl()
.instanceOf() for cross-window checks
await revealLeaf(), activeDocument only
Added npm run lint (Obsidian + TypeScript ESLint)
Typed FsModule / PathModule wrappers
Safe parsePluginData() / settings parsing
Removed unused ipcRenderer
Logger + console forwarding: warn/error only
Note
Direct fs access is still intentional for local file:// apps
releases.name: Local HTML Browser 1.0.3

releases.author: pranavshantagiri
releases.description:
Local HTML Browser 1.0.2
Requires Obsidian 1.7.2+ · Desktop only

Changes in 1.0.2
This release fixes Obsidian community plugin review feedback from 1.0.1. No new features — compliance, stability, and code quality.

**Requirements**
Raised minAppVersion from 1.5.0 to 1.7.2
Obsidian API compliance
Platform API instead of navigator.platform
getActiveViewOfType / getLeaf(false) instead of deprecated activeLeaf
Settings headings use Setting().setHeading() instead of raw tags
Removed inline element.style — UI state via CSS classes (is-loading, is-visible, is-hidden)
window.requestAnimationFrame() and activeDocument for popout window compatibility
Floating promises explicitly marked with void
**Source code / lint fixes**
Removed all eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any directives
Typed Electron/Node access via nodeRequire() with runtime validation
Safe parsing for plugin data, web page view state, and page note frontmatter
Webview event listeners without unnecessary type casts
Settings compat Refresh updates the report in place
**CSS**
Removed !important and scrollbar-width
Loading indicator, error banner, and status bar visibility handled in CSS
Build tooling
Dropped deprecated builtin-modules; esbuild uses Node's builtinModules
Expected remaining warnings
Direct fs access is intentional for local file:// apps outside the vault
releases.name: Local HTML Browser 1.0.2

releases.author: pranavshantagiri
releases.description:
Local HTML Browser v1.0.1
Run local file:// HTML apps inside Obsidian — SPAs, hash routes (#/today), offline dashboards, that kind of thing.

Desktop only · Obsidian 1.5.0+

Install
Download main.js, manifest.json, styles.css
Put them in .obsidian/plugins/local-html-browser/
Enable Local HTML Browser in Community plugins
Click the globe in the ribbon
Highlights
Full browser view — tabs, navigation, bookmarks, history, DevTools
Page tabs — live page as its own tab, no browser chrome
.webpage notes — save and reopen URLs from your vault
Auto-refresh when files change
Webview engine with automatic iframe fallback
Example URL
file:///C:/path/to/app/index.html#/today
Known limits
Webview needs your Obsidian build to support it — otherwise iframe fallback (SPAs may break)
Mobile not supported
Changelog
First release as local-html-browser
Browser view, page tabs, .webpage notes, live reload, compatibility report
releases.name: BrowserView-obsidian-plugin

releases.author: pranavshantagiri
releases.description:
# BrowserView v1.0.0

This is the first public release of BrowserView.

The goal of this project is simple: bring a proper browser into Obsidian. Whether you're previewing local HTML files, testing small web projects, or just want to keep everything inside your workspace, BrowserView is intended to make that possible.

This first release provides the basic browsing experience, including:

* Opening local `file://` HTML files
* Navigating to local and remote URLs
* Back and forward navigation
* Page reloading
* A simple address bar
* A browser view that integrates with the Obsidian workspace

BrowserView is still in its early stages, so you may encounter bugs or unsupported features. Some browser behavior is also limited by Electron and the Obsidian plugin environment, which means it won't always behave exactly like Chrome or Edge.

Future releases will focus on improving compatibility, adding developer-focused features, and making BrowserView feel more like a complete browser inside Obsidian.

If you run into any issues or have suggestions, please open an issue on GitHub. Feedback at this stage is especially valuable and will help guide the project's direction.
releases.name: BrowserView-obsidian-plugin
